articles.title: India-Israel BIA decoded: What's in it for businesses and investors?
articles.summary: The India-Israel Bilateral Investment Agreement (BIA), signed in September 2025, has come into force from July 4, 2026, replacing the nearly three-decade-old investment treaty between the two countries.

articles.title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad bullet train: India to get its 1st high-speed rail service by 2027; check key features, travel time & more
articles.summary: India's first bullet train is set to begin its journey by 2027, with the Surat to Bilimora section slated for inauguration. Union Railway Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw announced that construction on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad corridor has surpassed 80% completion.

articles.title: Year after calling India 'dead economy', Trump says country 'doing very well at 7-8%'
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump has lauded India's robust economic growth, a significant departure from his past criticisms. He cited India's 7-8% growth as an example of strong performance that shouldn't be stifled by high interest rates.

articles.title: India eyes $1 trillion exports milestone: Amid global turmoil, which sectors will drive growth? Decoded
articles.summary: During the April-June quarter, exports saw a 15% growth in merchandise shipments and around 11% in services exports. Which sectors have contributed to exports growth? What role has the PLI scheme played and will trade deals help expedite growth? We decode:

articles.title: India allows four Chinese firms into critical power tenders; Congress attacks move
articles.summary: India has permitted four Chinese power equipment manufacturers with local production to bid on critical government power projects for two years. This relaxation, aimed at accelerating power network expansion and renewable energy integration, follows a power ministry request.

articles.title: Fuel prices explained: Why oil companies are still losing ₹19 on diesel and ₹6 on petrol
articles.summary: State-run oil marketing companies (OMCs) lost an estimated ₹18.9 on every litre of diesel and ₹6 on every litre of petrol during the April-June quarter, even as pump prices remained largely unchanged.

articles.title: 'The Trump economy is soaring': US Prez cites stock market rally, tax cuts ahead of I-Day
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump declared the American economy is "soaring," citing record stock market highs, increased exports, and investment as proof of a new growth era.

articles.title: ‘It’s an epidemic now!’: Tesla batteries keep getting stolen; here’s what’s happening
articles.summary: Law enforcement officials are particularly concerned by the rise of so-called strategic thefts, in which organised groups exploit weaknesses in supply chain security rather than stealing unattended trailers.

articles.title: Karnataka HC directs Swiggy, Zomato, Zepto and others to deposit gig workers' welfare fee within 3 weeks
articles.summary: Refusing to grant an interim stay on the Karnataka Gig Workers Welfare Act, the Karnataka High Court has directed Swiggy, Zomato, Zepto and other app-based aggregators to deposit the disputed welfare fee with the court within three weeks.

articles.title: Online food delivery isn't slowing down: Why Indians are ordering more than ever
articles.summary: Online food delivery is set to become one of the biggest growth engines of India's food services industry, with consumers ordering more frequently than ever before.

articles.title: Quote of the Day by Rakesh Jhunjhunwala: ‘I'd be lying if I said I didn't like...’
articles.summary: Rakesh Jhunjhunwala (1960-2022) was a legendary Indian billionaire investor and stock trader often referred to as the “Big Bull” of India or the “Indian Warren Buffett”.

articles.title: AI boom lifts industrial engineers' pay packages
articles.summary: India's burgeoning AI data-centre sector is creating a surge in demand for mechanical, electrical, and industrial engineers. These professionals, once overlooked, are now commanding significant salaries as companies build energy-intensive AI infrastructure.

articles.title: HCLTech wins $1.1bn AI-led deal with Mercedes
articles.summary: HCLTech's stock surged almost 6% following the announcement of a massive $1.1 billion deal with a European Fortune Global 50 company, widely believed to be Mercedes-Benz.

articles.title: Identify imported goods for manufacturing: Piyush Goyal tells states
articles.summary: Commerce Minister Piyush Goyal has called on states to identify import-substitutable products, aiming to boost domestic manufacturing, cut foreign exchange outgo, and strengthen supply chains.

articles.title: SAP tightens hiring, travel as investments in AI zoom
articles.summary: German software giant SAP is implementing hiring freezes, travel restrictions, and tighter control over external spending to fuel its significant investments in artificial intelligence.

articles.title: Nearly 500 cities set to become next economic hubs: FM Sitharaman
articles.summary: India's middle class, comprising 31% of the population, is identified as the nation's primary growth engine, driving nearly all consumer spending. This demographic is expanding annually at 6.3% and is increasingly found in tier-two and tier-three cities, not just metropolises.

articles.title: Samsung phones lead in purchase via EMI route
articles.summary: Contrary to popular belief, Samsung leads India's smartphone sales via easy monthly installments, outpacing even Apple. Vivo and Oppo also show strong performance in this growing financing market.

articles.title: Factory floor emerges as new cyber battleground
articles.summary: Recent cyberattacks on Bajaj Auto and Tata Electronics have underscored the escalating cyber risks for India's manufacturing sector. As factories embrace automation and AI, their increased connectivity creates larger vulnerabilities.

articles.title: Loan growth speeds up in Q1, deposits still lag
articles.summary: Indian banks witnessed robust credit growth in the June 2026 quarter, with some lenders seeing advances surge significantly. However, a widening gap emerged as deposits declined for select banks like Bank of Baroda, IDBI, and RBL.

articles.title: PM Modi shares glimpse of new Jodhpur airport ahead of inauguration - see pics
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the new Jodhpur airport terminal, a significant boost for tourism and connectivity, blending modern design with Rajasthani heritage.

articles.title: India set to contest US tariff move, calls 'forced labour' findings legally flawed
articles.summary: India is set to contest proposed US tariffs on its exports next week, arguing that the US Trade Representative's findings on alleged forced labour are legally flawed.

articles.title: India sets $1 trillion export goal for FY26 as merchandise, services trade rise
articles.summary: India aims for a monumental $1 trillion in exports this fiscal year, a goal enthusiastically embraced by industry and policymakers. Union Minister Piyush Goyal confirmed the ambitious target, highlighting strong growth in both merchandise and services sectors.

articles.title: India sets $1 trillion export target for FY27, eyes 17% growth in merchandise exports
articles.summary: Piyush Goyal urges states, industry to make exports top priority, utilise FTAs

articles.title: The wait is over: Hyundai and Kia's most affordable electric cars are coming with 400KM+ single-charge range
articles.summary: Kia Syros EV launches July 2026 and Hyundai's budget electric SUV arrives in early 2027 — both with India-made LFP batteries and 400+ km range. Here's everything we know.

articles.title: Cancer pill costs ₹85,000 in US vs ₹35 in India? Woman says Americans getting ‘robbed’
articles.summary: The video quickly gained traction on social media, attracting more than 772,000 views and hundreds of comments. Many users echoed her concerns about high drug prices in the US, while others praised India's pharmaceutical industry and availability of lower-cost generic medicines.

articles.title: Centre cracks whip on Meta over Instagram ads promoting child sexual abuse material
articles.summary: India's IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw has ordered officials to summon Meta following reports of Instagram advertisements featuring child sexual abuse material.

articles.title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
articles.summary: Indian equity benchmarks closed higher on Friday, buoyed by positive global sentiment, falling crude oil prices, and anticipation of a softer interest rate environment. The Nifty 50 and BSE Sensex both saw gains, with the Nifty 50 ending the week nearly 1% up.

articles.title: What do you actually eat on a Vande Bharat train? Here's the complete food guide
articles.summary: From morning idli and chai to vegetarian thalis, chicken curry and gulab jamun, here's a complete look at what Vande Bharat Express serves passengers across its many routes.

articles.title: India’s women-owned business map has a western tilt
articles.summary: Women's entrepreneurship is growing in India, but unevenly. Cities in Gujarat and Maharashtra lead in female-owned businesses, while northern cities lag. This disparity highlights the complex interplay of economic structure, local conditions, and societal norms.

articles.title: Celebrity Shock: Swift & Kelce Fortune — How Pop Meets Sports in a Billion-Dollar Power Couple
articles.summary: Taylor Swift hits $2B net worth, Travis Kelce $70M; together, they merge music and sports, shaping culture, branding, and billion-dollar celebrity influence worldwide.

articles.title: Amazon Prime Day — Sennheiser Deals Transform Audio for Indian Shoppers
articles.summary: Amazon Prime Day 2026 offers up to 40% off Sennheiser headphones, microphones, and soundbars, with no-cost EMI and bank discounts, delivering premium audio to Indian shoppers.

articles.title: Regional connectivity to get big boost as PM to launch modified UDAN scheme
₹35K Crore Maruti Plant — India’s Auto Ambitions Hit High Gear
articles.summary: Maruti Suzuki inaugurates ₹35,000 crore Kharkhoda plant in Haryana with 500,000-unit capacity, advanced robotics, and plans to scale to 1 million, boosting India’s auto industry.

articles.title: Prime EV Deal — Hyundai Creta Offers Battery Rental and Long-Range Variant
articles.summary: Hyundai Creta EV launches in India with BaaS battery rental from ₹3.9/km, 420–510 km range, fast charging, dual screens, panoramic sunroof, ADAS safety, and multiple drive modes.

articles.title: Oppo Reno 16 Series — Premium Specs Hit India at Disruptive Prices
articles.summary: Oppo Reno 16 series launches in India with premium specs, massive batteries, quad cameras, AMOLED displays, and advanced processors starting at ₹46,999 with 10% cashback offers.

articles.title: Your mobile network could be at risk: ₹1 crore tower theft racket busted, devices smuggled abroad
articles.summary: Gang's high‑tech haul worth Rs 1 crore is seized as police foil racket that shipped stolen tower gear to Thailand and Hong Kong.

articles.title: BT Explainer: Why Bank of Baroda paid $600 mn to end a years-long NMC Health dispute
articles.summary: Shares of Bank of Baroda have fallen more than 7% in two sessions after the settlement news with NMC Health, once one of the UAE's largest private healthcare providers founded by NRI businessman BR Shetty

articles.title: ₹9,330 crore unclaimed in 30.9 lakh dormant EPF accounts, enough to build 3 IITs: RTI
articles.summary: More than ₹9,330 crore remains unclaimed in 30.91 lakh dormant EPF accounts, according to an RTI response accessed by India Today. The findings come even as the government rolls out the new EPF Scheme, 2026 to modernise provident fund services and improve claim settlements.

articles.title: Rs 15 order, Rs 500 tip: Blinkit rider thanks customer in emotional video, 'I don't even...'
articles.summary: In the clip, the rider turns the camera toward his phone to show viewers what had just come through. "This was a Rs 15 order. You know how much Didi tipped? She gave me a Rs 500 tip," he says, visibly moved

articles.title: What makes Zerodha what it is? Founder Nithin Kamath answers in the simplest way possible
articles.summary: Nithin Kamath said that he is often asked this one particular question when he is meeting young entrepreneurs.
